key_corrections: 68% semiconductor claim conflates two figures: 68% of E&E exports to US are semiconductors, and 65% of those come from US companies. Not '68% of semiconductor export revenue from US firms'.

'No standardized MYR options or futures on Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Market' is INCORRECT — Bursa lists MYR-denominated derivatives including KLCI Options and CPO Futures Options. Correct claim: Malaysia lacks deep offshore/onshore MYR currency-derivatives ecosystem.
82% global USD-invoiced trade figure not verified from FMM source. ECB data says USD + EUR together account for 80%+.
LCSF operational dates oversimplified: China from 2009, Thailand/Indonesia arrangements formalized mid-2010s then consolidated.
2.8% comparison is rhetorically loaded — compares cumulative stock with single-year flow.
Cross-border RMB Q1 2025 figure (102B yuan) is broader than Malaysia-side LCS total.
overall_assessment: Strongest on headline figures, country breakdown, BRICS status, swap sizes, and ringgit non-internationalization. Weaker on synthesis claims — one concrete market-structure claim is wrong, semiconductor exposure figure is conflated, and commodity pricing assertion lacks direct sourcing.
